folio.label: 144v
folio.l: For whil that Adam fasted , as I rede
He was in Paradys , and whan that he
Ete of the fruyt , defended , on a tree
A noon he was out cast , to woo & peyne
O glotonye , on the wel ougút vs pleyne
O wiste a man , how many maladies

note.notetxt: 185 O: I think there are two letters here, perhaps "So" or "Lo". Double check/ODR/JRT

folio.l: Folwen of excesse , and of glotonyes
He wolde ben , the more mesurable
Of his diete , sittyngö at his table
Allas the shorte throte , the tendre moutú
Maketú that Est and West , and Nortú & Soutú
In Erthe in Eyr in water , man to Swynke
To gete a gloton , deyntee mete and drynke
Of this matere , o Poule , wel kanstow trete
Mete vn to wombe , & wombe eke vn to mete
Shal god destroyen bothe , as Paulus seitú
Allas , a foul thyngö is it , by my feitú
To seye this worde , and foulere is the dede
Whan man so drynketú , of the white and rede
That of his throte , he maketú his pryuee
Thurgú thilke cursed , su¹fluitee
The Apostle wepyngö , seitú ful pitously
There walken manye , of whicú ©ow told haue I
I seye it now wepyngö , witú pitous vois
There ben enmys , of Cristes crois
Of whicú the ende is detú , wombe is here god
O wombe o bely , o stynkyngö Cod
Fulfild of donge , and of corrupcioun
At either ende of thee , foule is the soun
How gret labour and cost , is the to fynde
Theise Cookes , how they stampe & streyne & grynde
To tornen substaunce , in to Accident
To fulfille , al thy lykerous talent
Out of the harde bones , knokke they
The mary , for they caste nougút a wey
That may goo thurgú the golet , softe and swote
Of Spicerie , of leef of bark and roote
Shal ben his sause , I maked be delyt
To maken hi~ ©et , a newere appetit´
But certes , he that hauntetú swicú delices
Is ded , whil that he lyuetú in tho vices
¢ A lecherous thyngö is wyn , and dronkenesse
Is ful of stryuyngö , and of wrecchednesse
O dronke man , disfigured is thy face
Sour is thy bretú , foul artow to embrace
